mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals tighter monetary policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that support corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS exceeds 135,000 thousand.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 135,000 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects expanding economic demand that boosts corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 30% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal supply-side shocks that reduce corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL rises.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 200 signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate declines and equity market overvaluation, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1.4 million units indicate strong demand for construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1.4 million.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding supply capacity that lowers input costs, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 30% of periods with INDPRO growth > 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals tight labor markets that increase wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.5%.
